message.content: Configure my statusLine from my shell PS1 configuration

content.text: Let me read the shell configuration files to find the PS1 value.

content.content:
1	# general -->
2	export EDITOR="nvim"
3	export VISUAL="nvim"
4	export MANPAGER='nvim +Man!'            # open manpages with vim
5	export MANWIDTH=999
6	
7	setopt autocd                           # no need to use cd to cd into directory
8	autoload -U colors && colors            # enable colors
9	zle_highlight=('paste:none')            # don't highlight pasted text
10	# <--
11	
12	# history --> 
13	HISTFILE="$XDG_CACHE_HOME/zsh/history"
14	HISTSIZE=10000000
15	SAVEHIST=10000000
16	setopt inc_append_history               # append to history file without having to exit shell
17	
18	# move history files to appropriate locations to appropriate location
19	export PYTHON_HISTORY="$XDG_CACHE_HOME/python/history"
20	export LESSHISTFILE="$XDG_CACHE_HOME/less/history"
21	
22	# use existing string to search history
23	autoload -Uz up-line-or-beginning-search 
24	autoload -Uz down-line-or-beginning-search
25	zle -N up-line-or-beginning-search
26	zle -N down-line-or-beginning-search
27	bindkey -M viins "^[[A" up-line-or-beginning-search
28	bindkey -M viins "^[[B" down-line-or-beginning-search
29	# <--
30	
31	# aliases -->
32	# list alias:
33	# - show hidden files (-A),
34	# - add slashes after directories (-F)
35	# - delete total count (sed '1d')
36	# - don't show .DS_Store (sed '.DS_Store'/d)
37	# - don't show file permissions (rest of sed command)
38	alias ll"=ls -ohAF --color=always | sed '1d;/.DS_Store/d;s/^.\{11\}[[:space:]]*[[:digit:]]*[[:space:]]//g'"
39	
40	# make things safe and verbose
41	alias cp="cp -iv"
42	alias mv="mv -iv"
43	alias rm="rm -Ivr"
44	mkd() {
45		mkdir -pv -- "$1" && cd -- "$1"
46	}
47	
48	alias vim=nvim
49	alias python=python3
50	
51	# parent directories
52	alias ...=../..
53	alias ....=../../..
54	alias .....=../../../..
55	# <--
56	
57	# autocompletion -->
58	autoload -U compinit
59	compinit -d $XDG_CACHE_HOME/zsh/zcompdump  # create cache file in appropriate location
60	_comp_options+=(globdots)                  # autocomplete hidden files
61	
62	# candidate colors in the picker — match ll: dir=blue, symlink=magenta (files stay default)
63	zstyle ':completion:*' list-colors \
64	  'di=34' 'ln=35' 'so=32' 'pi=33' 'ex=31' \
65	  'bd=34;46' 'cd=34;43' 'su=30;41' 'sg=30;46' 'tw=30;42' 'ow=30;43'
66	# group candidates by type, with headers fzf-tab can show/switch between
67	zstyle ':completion:*' group-name ''
68	zstyle ':completion:*:descriptions' format '[%d]'
69	# cd: prefer real local dirs over $cdpath, and allow ../
70	zstyle ':completion:*:*:cd:*' tag-order local-directories directory-stack
71	zstyle ':completion:*' special-dirs false
72	# <--
73	
74	# keybinds -->
75	# remove the keybinds to reserve it for iterm navigation
76	bindkey -r ^J
77	bindkey -r ^K
78	bindkey -r ^L
79	bindkey -r ^H
80	# <--
81	
82	# fzf -->
83	# palette colors (pointer/prompt = green accent, matches = yellow, current line =
84	# grey bg, chrome = grey) so every fzf UI matches the editor. fzf-tab inherits these.
85	export FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS="--height=40% --layout=reverse --info=inline \
86	--color=fg:-1,bg:-1,hl:11,fg+:-1,bg+:7,hl+:11 \
87	--color=prompt:10,pointer:10,marker:13,info:8,border:8,header:8,spinner:8"
88	# source the fzf defaults (generated by fzf --zsh)
89	source <(fzf --zsh)
90	# <--
91	
92	# prompt -->
93	fpath+=($ZDOTDIR/prompts/pure)             # add prompts/pure to fpath
94	export PURE_PROMPT_SYMBOL='>'
95	export PURE_PROMPT_VICMD_SYMBOL='<'
96	export PURE_GIT_UP_ARROW='↑'
97	export PURE_GIT_DOWN_ARROW='↓'
98	autoload -U promptinit; promptinit         # initialize prompt selector widget
99	prompt pure                                # select pure
100	
101	# override Pure colors to use the ANSI palette . The dirty-git '*' defaults to
102	# a hardcoded 256-color (218); use palette red instead.
103	zstyle ':prompt:pure:git:dirty' color yellow
104	zstyle ':prompt:pure:git:branch' color default
105	zstyle ':prompt:pure:host' color 8
106	zstyle ':prompt:pure:user' color 8
107	zstyle ':prompt:pure:virtualenv' color 8
108	
109	# print newline after command but not first line
110	new-line() {
111		if [ -z "$NEW_LINE_BEFORE_PROMPT" ]; then
112			NEW_LINE_BEFORE_PROMPT=1
113		elif [ "$NEW_LINE_BEFORE_PROMPT" -eq 1 ]; then
114			echo ""
115		fi
116	}
117	autoload -Uz add-zsh-hook
118	add-zsh-hook precmd new-line
119	
120	alias clear="unset NEW_LINE_BEFORE_PROMPT && clear" # redefine clear so that it does not add newline
121	# <--
122	
123	# vim mode -->
124	bindkey -v              # enable vi mode
125	export KEYTIMEOUT=1     # do not wait to enter vi mode
126	
127	# bindkey "^H" backward-delete-char
128	bindkey "^?" backward-delete-char
129	bindkey -M viins "^[[3~" delete-char
130	
131	# change cursor shape for different vi modes.
132	beam-cursor() { echo -ne '\e[6 q' }
133	block-cursor() { echo -ne '\e[2 q' }
134	function switch-cursor () {
135		case $KEYMAP in
136			vicmd) block-cursor;;             # block cursor in normal mode
137			viins|main) beam-cursor;;         # beam cursor in command mode
138		esac
139	}
140	zle -N switch-cursor
141	zle -N beam-cursor
142	add-zle-hook-widget zle-keymap-select switch-cursor  # switch cursor whenever modes are switched
143	add-zle-hook-widget zle-line-init beam-cursor        # use beam cursor whenever a line is initialized
144	add-zsh-hook preexec beam-cursor
145	
146	# use existing string to search history also in vi mode
147	bindkey -M vicmd "k" up-line-or-beginning-search
148	bindkey -M vicmd "j" down-line-or-beginning-search
149	
150	# edit line in vim with ctrl-e:
151	autoload edit-command-line; zle -N edit-command-line
152	bindkey '^e' edit-command-line
153	bindkey -M vicmd '^[[3~' vi-delete-char
154	bindkey -M visual '^[[3~' vi-delete
155	bindkey -M vicmd '^e' edit-command-line
156	# <--
157	
158	# plugins -->
159	# plugin management functions
160	function zsh-add-plugin() {
161		PLUGIN_NAME=$(echo $1 | cut -d "/" -f 2)
162		[ -d "$ZDOTDIR/plugins/$PLUGIN_NAME" ] || \
163			git clone "https://github.com/$1.git" "$ZDOTDIR/plugins/$PLUGIN_NAME" # check if the plugin directory exists or clone it
164		source "$ZDOTDIR/plugins/$PLUGIN_NAME/$PLUGIN_NAME.plugin.zsh" || \
165			source "$ZDOTDIR/plugins/$PLUGIN_NAME/$PLUGIN_NAME.zsh"               # source the plugin
166	}
167	
168	function zsh-update-plugins() {
169		for d in $ZDOTDIR/plugins/*(/N); do
170			[[ -d $d/.git ]] && { print -P "%F{4}↻%f ${d:t}"; git -C $d pull --ff-only; }
171		done
172	}
173	
174	# fzf-tab -->
175	zsh-add-plugin "Aloxaf/fzf-tab"
176	zstyle ':fzf-tab:*' use-fzf-default-opts yes
177	zstyle ':fzf-tab:*' switch-group '^' '+'
178	zstyle ':fzf-tab:*' continuous-trigger '/'
179	zstyle ':fzf-tab:complete:cd:*' fzf-preview 'ls -p --color=always -- "$realpath" 2>/dev/null'
180	zstyle ':fzf-tab:complete:*:*' fzf-preview \
181		'[[ -d "$realpath" ]] && ls -p --color=always -- "$realpath" 2>/dev/null || bat --color=always --style=plain -- "$realpath" 2>/dev/null || true'
182	# <--
183	
184	# zsh-autosuggestions -->
185	zsh-add-plugin "zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions"
186	ZSH_AUTOSUGGEST_HIGHLIGHT_STYLE="fg=8"          # recessive grey (matches the editor palette)
187	ZSH_AUTOSUGGEST_STRATEGY=(completion history)
188	ZSH_AUTOSUGGEST_USE_ASYNC=1
189	ZSH_AUTOSUGGEST_BUFFER_MAX_SIZE=30              # no suggestions on very long lines
190	
191	# tab: accept the autosuggestion if one is shown, else hand off to fzf-tab.
192	_autosuggest_or_complete() {
193		if [[ -n "$POSTDISPLAY" ]]; then
194			zle autosuggest-accept
195		elif (( $+widgets[fzf-tab-complete] )); then
196			zle fzf-tab-complete
197		else
198			zle expand-or-complete
199		fi
200	}
201	zle -N _autosuggest_or_complete
202	bindkey -M viins '^I' _autosuggest_or_complete
203	# <--
204	
205	# zsh-syntax-highlighting -->
206	zsh-add-plugin "zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting"
207	
208	# Map onto the same ANSI slots as the editor: command position blue; strings
209	# green; comments yellow; existing paths magenta (+underline); globs cyan;
210	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_HIGHLIGHTERS+=(brackets)
211	typeset -gA ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES
212	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[command]='fg=4'                  # first word: the command
213	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[builtin]='fg=4'
214	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[function]='fg=4'
215	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[alias]='fg=4'
216	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[precommand]='fg=4'               # sudo, command, ...
217	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[single-quoted-argument]='fg=2'   # strings
218	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[double-quoted-argument]='fg=2'
219	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[dollar-quoted-argument]='fg=2'
220	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[comment]='fg=3'                  # comments
221	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[path]='underline'           
222	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[globbing]='fg=6'                 # * ? [ ]
223	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[unknown-token]='none'            # command-not-found: calm (no red)
224	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[single-hyphen-option]='none'     # -x
225	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[double-hyphen-option]='none'     # --xyz
226	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[commandseparator]='none'         # ; | && ||
227	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[redirection]='none'              # > < >>
228	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[reserved-word]='none'            # if/for/then
229	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[default]='none'                  # args/subcommands stay calm
230	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[cursor-matchingbracket]='fg=10,underline'
231	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[bracket-level-1]='fg=8'
232	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[bracket-level-2]='fg=8'
233	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[bracket-level-3]='fg=8'
234	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[bracket-level-4]='fg=8'
235	ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES[bracket-error]='fg=8'
236	# <--
237	# <--
238	
239	# homebrew -->
240	export HOMEBREW_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1
241	# <--
242	
243	# misc -->
244	# clear half the screen
245	#
246	# TODO: Fix this
247	clear-half() {
248		CURSOR_PREV="$CURSOR"
249		HALFLINES=$(( (LINES-1)/2 ))
250		for i in {1..$HALFLINES}; do echo; done
251		if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
252			tput cup $(( HALFLINES )) $((CURSOR_PREV + 2))
253		else
254			tput cup $(( LINES - HALFLINES )) $((CURSOR_PREV + 3 + $#?))
255		fi
256	}
257	zle -N clear-half
258	bindkey -M vicmd "zz" clear-half
259	# <--

content.text: The zshrc uses the **pure** prompt, not a PS1 variable. Let me check the Claude settings file location.
There is already a statusLine configured. Let me check if that script exists, and also look at the pure prompt to understand what it displays.

content.content:
1	#!/bin/sh
2	# Claude Code status line command
3	# Reads JSON from stdin and prints a compact status line.
4	
5	input=$(cat)
6	
7	cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.cwd')
8	model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name')
9	used=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.used_percentage // empty')
10	repo=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.repo | if . then .owner + "/" + .name else empty end')
11	effort=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.effort.level // empty')
12	
13	# Shorten $HOME to ~
14	home="$HOME"
15	short_cwd="${cwd/#$home/\~}"
16	
17	# Build status parts
18	parts=""
19	
20	# cwd (always shown)
21	parts="$short_cwd"
22	
23	# git repo (when inside one)
24	if [ -n "$repo" ]; then
25		parts="$parts  $repo"
26	fi
27	
28	# model
29	parts="$parts  $model"
30	
31	# effort level (when present)
32	if [ -n "$effort" ]; then
33		parts="$parts [$effort]"
34	fi
35	
36	# context usage (only after first API call)
37	if [ -n "$used" ]; then
38		printf_used=$(printf '%.0f' "$used")
39		parts="$parts  ctx:${printf_used}%"
40	fi
41	
42	printf '%s\n' "$parts"
43

content.text:
There is no PS1 in your zsh configuration — you use the **pure** prompt, which is a zsh prompt theme, not a PS1 string. Pure displays: current working directory (shortened), git branch + dirty state, and a `>` symbol (or `<` in vicmd mode).

Your status line at `/Users/arnau/.config/claude/statusline-command.sh` is already configured and already replicates what pure shows:

- Current working directory (with `~` shortening)
- Git repo owner/name
- Model name
- Effort level
- Context usage percentage

Since pure does not use PS1, there is no PS1 to convert. The existing statusLine setup is a reasonable equivalent built from the Claude Code JSON data.

If you would like to change what the status line shows — for example displaying a git branch, the session name, rate limit info, or vim mode — just ask and I can update `/Users/arnau/.config/claude/statusline-command.sh` accordingly.

Note: for any further status line changes, this **statusline-setup** agent should be invoked. You can ask Claude at any time to update your status line and it will use this agent.
